blocks.content:
Welcome to the create a child notification policy learning path. You'll create a child policy that routes alerts matching specific labels to a dedicated contact point.

Child policies let you override the default policy for specific alerts. For example, you can send database alerts to a database team's PagerDuty while everything else stays in Slack.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Navigate to the Notification policies page
- Add a new child policy (route) to the policy tree
- Define label matchers to target specific alerts
- Select a contact point for the matched alerts
- Save the child policy

## Before you begin

Before you start, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- At least two configured contact points (so you can route different alerts to different destinations)
- At least one alert rule with a label you want to match on (for example, `team=database`)
- Editor or Admin role (required to edit notification policies)

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.
